questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The relationship between the variables $r$ and $y$ is defined by an exponential equation. Which equation represents this relationship?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Jasmin grows bean pods in her garden. This year, she harvested 330 bean pods and saved 10% of them to plant next year. How many of the harvested bean pods did Jasmin save to plant next year?
questions.questionText: Jasmin grows bean pods in her garden. This year, she harvested 330 bean pods and saved 10% of them to plant next year. How many of the harvested bean pods did Jasmin save to plant next year?

questions.questionText: A sphere has a radius of $\frac{8}{3}$ feet. What is the volume, in cubic feet, of the sphere?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A company that manufactures staplers calculates its monthly profit, in dollars, by subtracting its fixed monthly costs, in dollars, from its monthly sales revenue, in dollars. The equation $11,000 = 2.00x - 6,500$ represents this situation for a month where $x$ staplers are manufactured and sold. Which statement is the best interpretation of $2.00x$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A company that manufactures staplers calculates its monthly profit, in dollars, by subtracting its fixed monthly costs, in dollars, from its monthly sales revenue, in dollars. The equation $11,000 = 2.00x - 6,500$ represents this situation for a month where $x$ staplers are manufactured and sold. Which statement is the best interpretation of $2.00x$ in this context?

choices.text: The monthly sales revenue, in dollars, from selling $x$ staplers
The monthly sales revenue, in dollars, from each stapler sold
The monthly cost, in dollars, of manufacturing each stapler
The monthly cost, in dollars, of manufacturing $x$ staplers

questions.questionText: If $\frac{x-16}{27}=\frac{x-16}{9}$, what is the value of x + 16?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The graph of line $h$ is shown in the $xy$-plane. Line $k$ (not shown) is defined by $sx + 40y = t$, where $s$ and $t$ are constants. If line $k$ is graphed in this $xy$-plane, the result is a graph of two linear equations. This system of two linear equations has no solution. Which of the following is NOT a possible value of $t$?
questions.questionText: The graph of line $h$ is shown in the $xy$-plane. Line $k$ (not shown) is defined by $sx + 40y = t$, where $s$ and $t$ are constants. modules.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
modules.subject: math

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$900 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $21h + 10c = 900$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 21 in this context?
questions.questionText: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$900 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $21h + 10c = 900$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 21 in this context?

choices.text: The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her regular job
The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her second job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her regular job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her second job

replacement.sourceTestName: March 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2

questions.testName: March 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The number of bacteria in a liquid medium doubles every day. There are 48,000 bacteria in the liquid medium at the start of an observation. Which of the following represents the number of bacteria, $y$, in the liquid medium $t$ days after the start of the observation?
questions.questionText: The number of bacteria in a liquid medium doubles every day. There are 48,000 bacteria in the liquid medium at the start of an observation. Which of the following represents the number of bacteria, $y$, in the liquid medium $t$ days after the start of the observation?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: At a convention center, there are a total of 325 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is 0.64, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is 0.32. How many visitors are located in room C?
questions.questionText: At a convention center, there are a total of 325 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is 0.64, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is 0.32. How many visitors are located in room C?

$y=\frac{x}{3}+8$
$y=-\frac{x}{3}+20$
The solution to the given system of equations is (x, y). What is the value of 2y?
questions.questionText:

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: An object is launched from a height of 124 feet above the ground. A quadratic function models the height above the ground, in feet, of the object $t$ seconds after it is launched. According to the model, 3 seconds after the object is launched, it reaches a maximum height of 268 feet above the ground. Which equation defines $f$?
questions.questionText: An object is launched from a height of 124 feet above the ground. A quadratic function models the height above the ground, in feet, of the object $t$ seconds after it is launched. According to the model, 3 seconds after the object is launched, it reaches a maximum height of 268 feet above the ground. Which equation defines $f$?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The graph of $ y = f(x) $ is shown, where $ f(x) = ab^2 + c $, and $ a $, $ b $, and $ c $ are constants. For how many values of $ x $ does $ f(x) = 0 $?
questions.questionText: The graph of $ y = f(x) $ is shown, where $ f(x) = ab^2 + c $, and $ a $, $ b $, and $ c $ are constants. For how many values of $ x $ does $ f(x) = 0 $?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The equation $h=\frac{9(u-273.15)}{5}+32$ gives the corresponding temperature $h$, in degrees Fahrenheit, of any substance that has a temperature of $u$ kelvins, where $u > 0$. If a substance has a temperature of 809.33 degrees Fahrenheit, what is the corresponding temperature, in kelvins, of this substance?

questions.prompt:
$x(r - 7) + 3 = 17x + 25$

questions.questionText:

questions.prompt: In the figure shown, $\overline{WZ}$ and $\overline{XY}$ intersect at point Q, YQ = 27, WQ = 90, WX = 60, and XQ = 110. What is the length of $\overline{YZ}$?
questions.questionText: In the figure shown, $\overline{WZ}$ and $\overline{XY}$ intersect at point Q, YQ = 27, WQ = 90, WX = 60, and XQ = 110. What is the length of $\overline{YZ}$?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: $a$ is 2,184% of the sum of the positive numbers $b$ and $c$, and $b$ is 84% of $c$. What percent of $b$ is $a$?
questions.questionText: $a$ is 2,184% of the sum of the positive numbers $b$ and $c$, and $b$ is 84% of $c$. What percent of $b$ is $a$?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$7p^{4}=x^{\frac{5}{4}}$
In the given equation, $p > 1$ and $t > 1$. If $t = p^{2n-1}$, where $n$ is a constant, what is the value of $n$?

$7p^{4}=x^{\frac{5}{4}}$

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A painting studio is offering a promotion where the first class is free, the second class is half off the regular price, and the remaining classes are regularly priced. If the regular price of a class is \$26.40, which function f gives the total cost, in dollars, of x classes taken using this promotion, where $x > 2$?

questions.prompt: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. If a triangle has side lengths of 10 and 12, which inequality represents the possible lengths, $x$, of the third side of the triangle?

questions.prompt: Right rectangular prism $X$ is similar to right rectangular prism $Y$. The surface area of right rectangular prism $X$ is 55 square centimeters ($\text{cm}^2$), and the surface area of right rectangular prism $Y$ is 880 $\text{cm}^2$. The volume of right rectangular prism $Y$ is 1,600 cubic centimeters ($\text{cm}^3$). What is the sum of the volumes, in $\text{cm}^3$, of right rectangular prism $X$ and right rectangular prism $Y$?

The given equation relates the distance $d$, in inches, of an object from its starting position to the time $t$, in seconds, since the object started moving. What is the rate of change of the object's distance from its starting position over time?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: What is the slope of the graph of $y=\frac{1}{2}(15x+12)+3x$ in the $xy$-plane?
questions.questionText: What is the slope of the graph of $y=\frac{1}{2}(15x+12)+3x$ in the $xy$-plane?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The table shows three values of x and their corresponding values of f(x), where $f(x)=\frac{kx+45}{x+2}$ and k is a constant. What is the value of k?
questions.questionText: The table shows three values of x and their corresponding values of f(x), where $f(x)=\frac{kx+45}{x+2}$ and k is a constant. What is the value of k?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The function f is defined by $f(x)=\frac{x+11}{3}$, and $f(a)= 16$, where a is a constant. What is the value of a?
questions.questionText: The function f is defined by $f(x)=\frac{x+11}{3}$, and $f(a)= 16$, where a is a constant. What is the value of a?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Jasmin grows bean pods in her garden. This year, she harvested 330 bean pods and saved 10% of them to plant next year. How many of the harvested bean pods did Jasmin save to plant next year?
questions.questionText: Jasmin grows bean pods in her garden. This year, she harvested 330 bean pods and saved 10% of them to plant next year. How many of the harvested bean pods did Jasmin save to plant next year?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A sphere has a radius of $\frac{8}{3}$ feet. What is the volume, in cubic feet, of the sphere?
questions.questionText: A sphere has a radius of $\frac{8}{3}$ feet. What is the volume, in cubic feet, of the sphere?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A company that manufactures staplers calculates its monthly profit, in dollars, by subtracting its fixed monthly costs, in dollars, from its monthly sales revenue, in dollars. The equation $11,000 = 2.00x - 6,500$ represents this situation for a month where $x$ staplers are manufactured and sold. Which statement is the best interpretation of $2.00x$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A company that manufactures staplers calculates its monthly profit, in dollars, by subtracting its fixed monthly costs, in dollars, from its monthly sales revenue, in dollars. The equation $11,000 = 2.00x - 6,500$ represents this situation for a month where $x$ staplers are manufactured and sold. Which statement is the best interpretation of $2.00x$ in this context?

choices.text: The monthly sales revenue, in dollars, from selling $x$ staplers
The monthly sales revenue, in dollars, from each stapler sold
The monthly cost, in dollars, of manufacturing each stapler
The monthly cost, in dollars, of manufacturing $x$ staplers

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: If $\frac{x-16}{27}=\frac{x-16}{9}$, what is the value of x + 16?
questions.questionText: If $\frac{x-16}{27}=\frac{x-16}{9}$, what is the value of x + 16?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The graph of line $h$ is shown in the $xy$-plane. Line $k$ (not shown) is defined by $sx + 40y = t$, where $s$ and $t$ are constants. If line $k$ is graphed in this $xy$-plane, the result is a graph of two linear equations. This system of two linear equations has no solution. Which of the following is NOT a possible value of $t$?
questions.questionText: The graph of line $h$ is shown in the $xy$-plane. Line $k$ (not shown) is defined by $sx + 40y = t$, where $s$ and $t$ are constants. If line $k$ is graphed in this $xy$-plane, the result is a graph of two linear equations. This system of two linear equations has no solution. Which of the following is NOT a possible value of $t$?

questions.testName: December 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$|7 - x| + 1 = 81$
What is the sum of the solutions to the given equation?
questions.questionText:
$|7 - x| + 1 = 81$

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$900 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $21h + 10c = 900$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 21 in this context?
questions.questionText: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$900 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $21h + 10c = 900$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 21 in this context?

choices.text: The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her regular job
The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her second job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her regular job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her second job

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:

Which of the following equations best represents the line of best fit shown?

questions.prompt:

One of the two equations in a system of linear equations is given. The system has infinitely many solutions. If the second equation in the system is $y = mx + b$, where $m$ and $b$ are constants, what is the value of $b$?

replacement.sourceTestName: March 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
replacement.note: Slot 5 is usually Equivalent expressions in Math Module 2, so this module borrows a matching past-bank question.

questions.testName: March 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The number of bacteria in a liquid medium doubles every day. There are 48,000 bacteria in the liquid medium at the start of an observation. Which of the following represents the number of bacteria, $y$, in the liquid medium $t$ days after the start of the observation?
questions.questionText: The number of bacteria in a liquid medium doubles every day. There are 48,000 bacteria in the liquid medium at the start of an observation. Which of the following represents the number of bacteria, $y$, in the liquid medium $t$ days after the start of the observation?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: At a convention center, there are a total of 325 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is 0.64, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is 0.32. How many visitors are located in room C?
questions.questionText: At a convention center, there are a total of 325 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is 0.64, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is 0.32. How many visitors are located in room C?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$y=\frac{x}{3}+8$
$y=-\frac{x}{3}+20$
The solution to the given system of equations is (x, y). What is the value of 2y?
questions.questionText:

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: An object is launched from a height of 124 feet above the ground. A quadratic function models the height above the ground, in feet, of the object $t$ seconds after it is launched. According to the model, 3 seconds after the object is launched, it reaches a maximum height of 268 feet above the ground. Which equation defines $f$?
questions.questionText: An object is launched from a height of 124 feet above the ground. A quadratic function models the height above the ground, in feet, of the object $t$ seconds after it is launched. According to the model, 3 seconds after the object is launched, it reaches a maximum height of 268 feet above the ground. Which equation defines $f$?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The graph of $ y = f(x) $ is shown, where $ f(x) = ab^2 + c $, and $ a $, $ b $, and $ c $ are constants. For how many values of $ x $ does $ f(x) = 0 $?
questions.questionText: The graph of $ y = f(x) $ is shown, where $ f(x) = ab^2 + c $, and $ a $, $ b $, and $ c $ are constants. For how many values of $ x $ does $ f(x) = 0 $?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The equation $h=\frac{9(u-273.15)}{5}+32$ gives the corresponding temperature $h$, in degrees Fahrenheit, of any substance that has a temperature of $u$ kelvins, where $u > 0$. If a substance has a temperature of 809.33 degrees Fahrenheit, what is the corresponding temperature, in kelvins, of this substance?

questions.prompt:

questions.prompt: In the figure shown, $\overline{WZ}$ and $\overline{XY}$ intersect at point Q, YQ = 27, WQ = 90, WX = 60, and XQ = 110. What is the length of $\overline{YZ}$?

questions.prompt: $a$ is 2,184% of the sum of the positive numbers $b$ and $c$, and $b$ is 84% of $c$. What percent of $b$ is $a$?
questions.questionText: $a$ is 2,184% of the sum of the positive numbers $b$ and $c$, and $b$ is 84% of $c$. What percent of $b$ is $a$?

questions.prompt:
$7p^{4}=x^{\frac{5}{4}}$

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A painting studio is offering a promotion where the first class is free, the second class is half off the regular price, and the remaining classes are regularly priced. If the regular price of a class is \$26.40, which function f gives the total cost, in dollars, of x classes taken using this promotion, where $x > 2$?

questions.prompt: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. If a triangle has side lengths of 10 and 12, which inequality represents the possible lengths, $x$, of the third side of the triangle?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Right rectangular prism $X$ is similar to right rectangular prism $Y$. The surface area of right rectangular prism $X$ is 55 square centimeters ($\text{cm}^2$), and the surface area of right rectangular prism $Y$ is 880 $\text{cm}^2$. The volume of right rectangular prism $Y$ is 1,600 cubic centimeters ($\text{cm}^3$). What is the sum of the volumes, in $\text{cm}^3$, of right rectangular prism $X$ and right rectangular prism $Y$?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = \frac{|x|}{a} - 16$, where $a < 0$. What is the product of $f(17a)$ and $f(8a)$?
